sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the purple block at the top of the main area has changed to white! The top block is now white (with gray frame), and below it is the purple+white split block. So clicking on the white indicator in the top-right panel changed the top block's color from purple to white. This confirms that the top-right boxes are color selectors that paint the selected/active block.
Now I have:
- Top block: white (was purple)
- Bottom block: purple top half, white bottom half
The goal (top-left) shows: white on top, purple on bottom. So I need the bottom block to be fully purple.
Let me try clicking on the purple indicator to see if it changes the bottom block, or try SPACE to see what happens now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking white indicator changed the top block from purple to white!
# Now I need to make the bottom block fully purple.
# Let me try clicking the purple indicator to select purple, then SPACE to apply it.
# Purple indicator is at approximately row 3-5, col 42-44
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 43}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
